name: Bulgana Green Power Hub - Wind Farm
notable: 56 turbines. Neoen project with 15yr Victorian Govt support agreement. Nectar Farms 10yr PPA (collapsed). 20MW/34MWh BESS co-located.

timeline.title: Financial close
timeline.detail: Neoen reached financial close for wind+battery hybrid project

timeline.title: Construction commenced
timeline.detail: Construction began for 56 Vestas turbines + 20 MW/34 MWh battery

timeline.title: Commercial operations commenced
timeline.detail: Commencement date from AEMO registration via OpenElectricity.

timeline.title: First generation recorded
timeline.detail: First market dispatch data recorded in AEMO systems.

eis_specs.document_title: Bulgana Green Power Hub Environment Effects Statement (VIC DELWP EES)
eis_specs.hub_height_note: Standard 97 m hub height for V126-3.45 MW on Western VIC plateau
eis_specs.wind_speed_period: Mean annual wind speed at hub height; Bulgana area near Ararat, Western Victoria
eis_specs.energy_yield_method: WAsP with long-term correction; P50 net yield after wake and availability losses
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Bulgana 220 kV substation (on-site — wind farm anchors this substation)
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: Essentially on-site — Bulgana Green Power Hub is adjacent to the Bulgana 220 kV substation.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: Minimal — Bulgana substation was purpose-built as part of this project, then expanded for Golden Plains and Berrybank.
eis_specs.notes: 194 MW (56 x 3.45 MW Vestas V126), near Ararat, Western Victoria. Developer: Vena Energy. VIC EES approved 2018. Now operating. The Bulgana substation created by this project became the anchor connection point for the entire Western Victoria Wind Farm Zone.
